meaning: to not have; to not be there; to not exist

expressions.english: to have no money
to have no time
there is no way; to have no solution
to have no appetite

there is no abnormality
everything is fine
to have nothing to say

to not know anyone
there is no one who knows
I don't have anything to say.

There's no problem even if we do it like this?
Even if we do it like this, there's no problem?
expressions.notes:
이렇게 = like this, in this manner; so +adjective
-이/이/어도 = even though; no matter how much + S + V
문제 = problem; issue; thing; matter

expressions.english: These days, there is nothing very interesting going on.
expressions.notes:
요즘 = these days, lately
별로 = not particularly, not very, not so much, not really
재미있다 = to be fun; to be funny; to be interesting

expressions.english: I had no appetite, so I didn't have lunch.

expressions.korean: 가게에 사람이 아무도 없는 것 같아요.
expressions.english:
I think there is no one in the store.
I think nobody is in the store.
expressions.notes:
가게 = store, shop
아무도 = nobody; anybody (always used with a negative sentence structure)

expressions.korean: 그 사람은 성격이 나빠서 친구가 없어요.
expressions.english: He has a bad personality, so he doesn't have any friends.
expressions.notes:
성격 = personality, disposition
나쁘다 = to be bad

expressions.korean: 이 신발을 사고 싶은데 지금 돈이 없어요.
expressions.english: I want to buy these shoes, but I don't have any money (on me) now.

expressions.korean: 제가 없는 동안에 제 강아지 좀 돌봐 주세요.
expressions.english: While I'm not here, please look after my dog.
expressions.notes:
동안 = during; while; for + period of time
강아지 = puppy
돌봐다 = to take care of, look after
-이/이/여 주 세요 = (polite) Please do + something + for me

expressions.korean: 인사를 하고 싶었는데 기회가 없어서 못 했어요.
expressions.english: I wanted to say "hi," but I couldn't because I didn't have the opportunity.
expressions.notes:
인사 = greeting
인사하다 = to greet, say "hello"
기회 = chance, opportunity

expressions.english: I've lost my cellphone. Do you think there is a way I can find it?
expressions.notes:
핸드폰 = mobile phone
잃어버리다 = to lose + N

expressions.korean: 요즘에 회사에서 바빠서 영화 볼 시간이 없었어요.
expressions.english: I've been busy at work lately, so I haven't had time to watch movies.

expressions.korean: 카메라를 떨어뜨렸는데 다행하도 카메라에 아무 이상이 없었어요.
expressions.english: I dropped my camera, but fortunately there was nothing wrong with the camera.
expressions.notes:
카메라 = camera
떨어뜨리다 = to drop
다행하도 = fortunately
이상 = (not) any

expressions.korean: 어제 분명히 열쇠를 서랍 안에 놨는데 지금 찾아 보니까 없어요.
expressions.english: I am sure that I put the key in the drawer yesterday, but now I can't find it.
expressions.notes:
열쇠 = key
서랍 = drawer (of desk or dresser)
놓다 = to put (something) in
두다 = to leave (something) at a place
